sections.paragraph:
τροφοφορέω 1 aor. ἐτροφοφόρησα ‘carry in one’s arms’, i.e. care for τινά someone (tenderly) Ac 13:18 v.l. (fr. Dt 1:31 [s. τροποφορέω]. Cp. 2 Macc 7:27 and Macarius, Hom. 46, 3). See Beginn. I/4, 149.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. fr. = from

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Beginn. Beginn. = The Beginnings of Christianity, ed. FJFoakesJackson and KLake—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1017
